assignment.title: Types of Evidence and Fingerprint Analysis Assignment
assignment.instructor_text:
After a crime has occurred, criminal investigators use scientific techniques and/or forensic science experts to help identify and interpret any physical evidence from the crime scene. Physical evidence from a crime scene comes in many different forms, such as fingerprints, hair, blood, saliva, semen, skin, bone, bullets, bullet casings, paint fragments, and fibres. 

Individualized Physical Evidence is unique and can be directly linked to a specific person and/or source. Ex. fingerprints, DNA, bullets, dental impressions

Identified Physical Evidence shares a common source; it can be grouped into a class of items having similar properties. Ex. clothing, shoe prints, blood type

Complete the following assignment about crime scene procedures.  Click on the image below to make a copy of the Types of Evidence Assignment.  Remember to double click the top of the document to open the header and add your name to the document.

When you have done this, submit your assignment below by uploading your file and clicking the Submit button.

If you need a refresher on how to submit your assignments, you can access the steps in the Course Information section.
